 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2.2","title":"Allowlist: evaluator integration (override decision safely)","description":"## Objective

Integrate allowlist checks into the shared evaluator.

## Required behavior

- Evaluate packs and find the first high-confidence match.
- Compute the stable rule ID `pack_id:pattern_name`.
- Check allowlists after match identity is known.
- If allowlisted:
  - override DENY -> ALLOW (or DENY -> WARN if decision modes exist)
  - record the allowlist reason and layer for explain/logging

Safety rule:

- Allowlisting must only bypass the specific matched rule (or an allowed pack-scoped wildcard). It must not disable other packs.

## Edge cases

- Patterns with no `pattern_name`: define a policy (either forbid allowlisting or synthesize a stable name).
- Multiple matches: only the "winning" match should be allowlist-checkable (unless we later support multi-match reporting).

## Acceptance criteria

- Allowlisting a rule bypasses only that rule.
- Non-allowlisted dangerous commands remain blocked.
- Explain and logs can show the allowlist override (layer + entry).

## Tests

- Unit tests:
  - allowlist hit overrides deny
  - allowlist miss does not change decision
  - wildcard allowlist matches only within pack
- Integration test: end-to-end deny becomes allow when allowlisted.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2.3","title":"Allowlist CLI: add/list/remove/validate (project + user)","description":"## Objective

Implement `dcg allowlist` CLI commands so users can fix false positives without editing TOML by hand.

## Commands (v1)

Primary namespace:

- `dcg allowlist add <rule_id> --reason "..." [--project|--user] [--expires ...] [--condition KEY=VAL]`
- `dcg allowlist add-command "<cmd>" --reason "..." [--project|--user] [--expires ...]`
- `dcg allowlist list [--project|--user]`
- `dcg allowlist remove <rule_id|entry_id> [--project|--user]`
- `dcg allowlist validate [--project|--user]`

Ergonomic aliases (high-value):

- `dcg allow <rule_id> --reason "..." [--project|--user]` → alias of `allowlist add`
- `dcg unallow <rule_id|entry_id> [--project|--user]` → alias of `allowlist remove`

Rationale: deny messages can print shorter copy/paste commands.

## Behavioral requirements

- Default scope should be explicit:
  - either require `--project|--user`, or default to project if in a git repo and file exists
- All mutations must preserve TOML readability (stable ordering; keep comments where feasible).
- CLI should set audit metadata automatically:
  - `added_by` (from env/user)
  - `added_at` (RFC3339)

## Acceptance criteria

- CLI can create `.dcg/allowlist.toml` if missing.
- CLI mutations are idempotent and stable (no reordering chaos).
- Validate prints actionable errors/warnings.

## Tests

- CLI integration tests for add/list/remove (including alias commands).
- Validation tests for:
  - expired entries
  - missing reason
  - unknown rule id

All quality gates pass (463 tests, clippy, fmt).","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:04:59.51609388-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:21:56.560137459-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2.4","title":"Allowlist: conditions, expiration, and wildcard safety rules","description":"## Objective

Implement the more advanced allowlist semantics in a way that is safe and predictable.

## Semantics to support

### 1) Expiration

- `expires_at` field on entries.
- Expired entries:
  - evaluator: ignored
  - validate: warning (or error if `--strict`)

### 2) Conditions (environment gating)

- `conditions = { KEY = "VAL" }` maps to environment variables.
- Conditions are ANDed.
- Missing env var means condition not met.

### 3) Wildcards

- Allow `pack_id:*` (scoped to one pack).
- Disallow `*:*`.
- Allow `pack_id:prefix-*` only if we commit to stable naming conventions.

### 4) Risk acknowledgement

- Regex allowlist entries require `risk_acknowledged=true`.
- Validation should warn on overly broad regex (heuristics).

## Acceptance criteria

- Expiration works and is test-covered.
- Conditions work (CI-only allowlists etc).
- Wildcard constraints prevent unsafe global bypass.

## Tests

- Expiration tests using fixed timestamps.
- Condition tests with env var manipulation.
- Wildcard constraint tests.

-{"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2.5","title":"Allowlist: comprehensive tests + documentation","description":"## Objective

Make allowlisting safe in practice by shipping:

- comprehensive automated tests
- clear user documentation

## Tests

### Parser/validation

- invalid TOML does not crash hook
- missing required fields surfaced in validate
- risky entries require acknowledgement

### Evaluator integration

- allowlist overrides only the intended rule
- allowlisting does not disable other packs
- allowlist layer precedence works

### CLI

- add/list/remove idempotence
- validate output contains actionable messages

## Docs

- README section: "Resolving false positives" describing:
  - `dcg explain`
  - `dcg allowlist add ...`
  - recommended allowlist shapes
  - security caveats

## Acceptance criteria

- Tests cover the core allowlist behaviors and guard against regressions.
- Docs are sufficient that a user can resolve a false positive without reading source.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2.6","title":"Allowlist: E2E coverage in scripts/e2e_test.sh (hook path)","description":"## Objective

Add end-to-end coverage proving allowlists affect the **real hook path** (stdin JSON → decision) and do not create accidental broad bypasses.

## Why E2E is required

Allowlisting is a high-risk feature:

- It must override only the intended rule.
- It must work in hook mode (not just `dcg test`).
- It must not silently disable packs or normalization.

## Test cases (must include)

1) Baseline: a known catastrophic command is blocked
- e.g., `git reset --hard` (record the rule_id used)

2) Project allowlist overrides that exact rule
- Create `.dcg/allowlist.toml` in an isolated fixture repo/cwd.
- Re-run the same hook JSON input.
- Expected: ALLOW (or WARN if configured) with **no JSON deny**.

3) Non-target rule remains enforced
- Ensure another blocked command is still blocked (e.g., `git clean -fd`).

4) Expired allowlist entry does not apply

5) Unsafe wildcard entry is rejected (or requires risk_ack)

## Logging requirements

E2E output should print:

- the rule_id being allowlisted
- which allowlist layer was used (project vs user)
- expected vs actual decision

## Acceptance criteria

- E2E fails if allowlist does not apply in hook mode.
- E2E fails if allowlist is broader than intended.

## Dependencies

- `git_safety_guard-1gt.2.2` evaluator integration
- `git_safety_guard-1gt.2.3` allowlist CLI (optional; direct file edit acceptable in test)
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:23:47.275624855-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T17:58:02.311819693-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T17:58:02.311819693-05:00","close_reason":"Added 11 E2E tests for allowlist: rule overrides, expiration, wildcards, conditions, multi-entry matching","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2.6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:23:58.619659792-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2.6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:24:03.663391147-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2.6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:24:08.707714436-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3","title":"Add decision modes: block vs warn vs log-only","description":"## Objective

Reduce disruption by allowing less-confident detections to warn or log instead of hard-blocking, while keeping high-confidence matches as hard blocks.

## Background

Some detections are extremely high confidence (e.g., `rm -rf /`, `git reset --hard`). Others are context-dependent or susceptible to false positives.

A single binary mode (always deny) can be overly disruptive, especially when expanding into heredoc scanning and AST-based heuristics.

## Proposed behavior

Add a policy layer that can choose between:

- **deny** (current behavior): block command
- **warn**: print warning to stderr and/or log, but allow command (no JSON deny)
- **log-only**: silent allow, but log event for later review

Policy should be configurable:

- global default (e.g., deny)
- per-pack override
- per-pattern override

## Safety constraints

- Default should remain deny for known-catastrophic patterns.
- Warn/log-only must never apply to the most dangerous patterns unless the user explicitly configures it.

## Acceptance criteria

- Config supports selecting mode per pack/pattern.
- Output is correct per mode:
  - deny: stderr warning + stdout JSON deny
  - warn: stderr warning, no stdout
  - log-only: optional log entry, no stderr/stdout
- Tests cover all three modes.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:05:08.791980925-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T14:19:44.235316204-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T14:19:44.235316204-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented policy config (default/pack/rule) and hook behavior for deny/warn/log; added E2E policy coverage; Critical rules cannot be globally downgraded.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:05:08.793291122-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:06:02.265838837-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3.1","title":"Decision policy: define rule severity taxonomy + default modes","description":"## Objective

Define an explicit, documented severity taxonomy for rules, and map severity → default decision mode.

This prevents ad-hoc "some things warn, some deny" drift and supports:

- predictable rollout (warn-first where appropriate)
- stable CI policy (`--fail-on error|warning` for scan)
- consistent UX between hook, explain, and scan

## Proposed taxonomy (v1)

- `error` (catastrophic / irreversible): default **DENY**
  - examples: `rm -rf /`, `git reset --hard`, `git clean -fd`, `DROP DATABASE prod`

- `warning` (dangerous but sometimes intentional): default **WARN** (or DENY in strict packs)
  - examples: `git push --force` (could be warn if confidence low)

- `info` (advisory): default **LOG-ONLY**

## Rules of the road

- Severity is a property of a rule (pack_id + pattern_name), not of user configuration.
- User configuration can *tighten* (warn→deny) freely.
- User configuration can *loosen* (deny→warn/log) only with explicit acknowledgement (see allowlist safety rules).

## Acceptance criteria

- `Severity` enum exists and is used consistently across:
  - hook deny/warn/log output
  - `dcg explain`
  - `dcg scan` JSON output
- Default mapping is documented and test-covered.

## Dependencies

- `git_safety_guard-1gt.3` (decision modes implementation)
- `git_safety_guard-99e.2` (stable rule IDs)
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:01:58.171989907-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T01:50:42.026890924-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T01:50:42.026890924-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented Severity and DecisionMode enums with Critical/High/Medium/Low levels. Updated DestructivePattern, DestructiveMatch, CheckResult structs. Added 10 comprehensive tests. All 92 tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:02:07.49512402-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:02:19.24160358-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-7tg","title":"Epic: Test infrastructure + performance guardrails","description":"# Epic: Test infrastructure + performance guardrails

## Why this epic exists

`dcg` runs on every Bash command. That makes it more like a kernel hook than a typical CLI:

- it must never hang
- it must never panic
- it must have bounded worst-case latency

As we add complex logic (execution-context parsing, heredoc extraction, AST matching, repo scanning), the only sustainable way to maintain trust is to encode invariants and budgets in automated guardrails.

## Goals

1. Property-based tests for core invariants (determinism, idempotence, no panics).
2. Fuzzing harness for high-risk parsers/tokenizers/extractors.
3. Performance benchmarks with budgets (fast path and slow path) and CI enforcement.
4. Regression corpora for:
   - true positives
   - false positives
   - bypass attempts
   - edge cases

## Non-goals

- Chasing high coverage numbers at the expense of signal.
- Building a heavy telemetry system (optional later).

## Success Criteria

- CI can run a "core suite" quickly (unit + integration + key E2E) and a "deep suite" on schedule (fuzz/bench/proptest).
- Performance regressions are caught before release (budgets enforced, not just tracked).
- Any crash found by fuzzing is captured as a minimal repro and promoted into a regression test.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:07:59.839695889-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T22:51:02.770855141-05:00"}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.1","title":"Property-based tests for evaluator invariants (proptest)","description":"## Objective

Add property-based tests that encode the invariants we never want to regress.

## Target invariants

1. Normalization idempotence:
   - `normalize(normalize(cmd)) == normalize(cmd)`
2. Determinism:
   - evaluating the same input twice yields the same decision and match identity
3. No panics:
   - evaluation never panics for arbitrary UTF-8 input
4. Bounded behavior:
   - overly large inputs are handled without pathological allocations

## Scope

- Focus on the shared evaluator entrypoint.
- Keep strategies controlled to avoid CI flakiness.
- Mark the slowest tests `#[ignore]` and run them in a deeper CI job.

## Acceptance criteria

- Proptest suite exists and runs locally.
- CI can run a small proptest subset reliably.
- Any discovered bug results in a reduced regression test.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RusticPuma","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:12:38.559130823-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T00:46:28.670954152-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T00:46:28.670954152-05:00","close_reason":"Property tests implemented with proptest: (1) normalization_is_idempotent, (2) evaluation_is_deterministic, (3) evaluation_never_panics, (4) handles_large_inputs, (5) empty_and_whitespace_allowed. Fixed Cow borrow issue by using into_owned(). All 103 lib + 92 main + 140 E2E tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-7tg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:13:09.5251165-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:26:43.044726491-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:26:48.30731798-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.2","title":"Fuzzing harness for high-risk parsers/tokenizers (cargo-fuzz)","description":"## Objective

Add fuzz targets for the components most likely to panic or hang:

- hook JSON parsing
- shell tokenizer / execution-context classifier
- heredoc extractor
- shared evaluator entrypoint

## Requirements

- Use `cargo-fuzz` (nightly) with time-limited CI runs.
- Add at least these fuzz targets:
  - `evaluate` (full evaluator)
  - `hook_input` (JSON parser)
  - `tokenizer` (context classifier)
  - `heredoc_extract`

## Acceptance criteria

- Fuzz targets run locally.
- CI runs fuzzing for a bounded time (e.g., 5 minutes per target on schedule).
- Any found crash becomes a regression test.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:12:43.859065882-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T00:54:58.127555188-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T00:54:58.127555188-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented 4 fuzz targets (evaluate, hook_input, context, normalize). Fuzzing discovered a regex backtracking panic bug (git_safety_guard-hcj).","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-7tg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:13:14.712645803-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:26:53.558806407-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:26:58.789818571-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-t8x.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:27:04.036438019-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.3","title":"Performance budgets + benchmark enforcement","description":"## Objective

Codify performance budgets and enforce them in CI so `dcg` stays invisible.

## Budgets to encode

- Fast path (no heredoc/inline): p99 under 500us
- Slow path (heredoc/inline): p99 under 20ms
- Absolute max: fail-open beyond 50ms

These numbers can be revised, but must be explicit.

## Requirements

- Benchmarks for:
  - quick reject / keyword gating
  - normalization
  - pack evaluation
  - overrides/allowlist
  - heredoc trigger/extract/AST path
- A CI job that compares benchmarks to budgets (tolerant of variance).

## Acceptance criteria

- Budgets exist as code constants/config.
- CI can detect large regressions and fail.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:12:49.116455774-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T21:12:49.116455774-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-7tg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:13:19.941175632-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:27:09.296452284-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-perf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:27:14.532426804-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.3","title":"Performance budgets + benchmark enforcement","description":"## Objective

Codify performance budgets and enforce them in CI so `dcg` stays invisible.

## Budgets to encode

- Fast path (no heredoc/inline): p99 under 500us
- Slow path (heredoc/inline): p99 under 20ms
- Absolute max: fail-open beyond 50ms

These numbers can be revised, but must be explicit.

## Requirements

- Benchmarks for:
  - quick reject / keyword gating
  - normalization
  - pack evaluation
  - overrides/allowlist
  - heredoc trigger/extract/AST path
- A CI job that compares benchmarks to budgets (tolerant of variance).

## Acceptance criteria

- Budgets exist as code constants/config.
- CI can detect large regressions and fail.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.4","title":"CI workflows: core suite vs deep suite (fuzz/proptest/bench)","description":"## Objective

Add CI workflows that run the right tests at the right cadence.

## Core suite (per PR)

- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test`
- selected E2E script coverage

## Deep suite (scheduled or manual)

- proptest ignored tests
- fuzzing time-limited runs
- benchmarks with budget checks

## Acceptance criteria

- CI is fast enough for PR iteration.
- Deep suite can be run without human babysitting.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.5","title":"Regression corpus harness (true/false positives + bypass attempts)","description":"## Objective

Create and maintain a small, curated corpus of high-signal command cases and a harness that runs them through:

- hook JSON path
- CLI evaluator path

## Corpus categories

- `true_positives/` (must block)
- `false_positives/` (must allow)
- `bypass_attempts/` (must block or warn)
- `edge_cases/` (must not crash/hang)

## Output/logging requirements

- Harness prints per-case:
  - description
  - expected decision
  - actual decision
  - rule id when matched

## Acceptance criteria

- Corpus harness runs in CI.
- Adding a new regression is easy and does not require writing Rust test code each time.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-8wt","title":"Create comprehensive test suite for heredoc detection","description":"## Objective

Create comprehensive test coverage for heredoc detection and AST-based pattern matching, including unit tests, integration tests, and bypass attempt tests.

## Test Categories

### 1. Unit Tests

#### Heredoc Detection Tests

- Bash heredoc variants: `<<EOF`, `<<-EOF`, `<<<`, `<<'EOF'`, `<<"EOF"`
- Python inline scripts: `python3 -c ...`
- Node.js: `node -e ...`, `node <<EOF`
- Perl: `perl -e ...`, `perl <<'END'`
- Ruby: `ruby -e ...`, heredoc variants

#### Language Detection Tests

- Detection from command prefix (python3, node, ruby, perl, bash)
- Detection from shebang (`#!/usr/bin/env python3`)
- Detection from content heuristics (bounded)
- Fallback behavior for unknown languages

#### Pattern Matching Tests

- Each destructive pattern per language (high-signal)
- Each safe/negative pattern (things that look dangerous but aren't)
- Edge cases in quoting and escaping

### 2. Integration Tests

#### Pipeline Tests

- Full flow from JSON input to block/allow decision
- Heredoc commands correctly trigger deep analysis
- Non-heredoc commands still use fast path
- Performance within latency budget

#### Error Handling Tests

- Malformed heredoc syntax
- Unparseable content
- Unknown languages fall back gracefully
- Timeout handling (fail-open in hook mode)

### 3. Bypass Attempt Tests

Curate a set of realistic bypass attempts we want to cover, e.g.:

- variable indirection (`CMD=rm; $CMD -rf ...`)
- command substitution (`$(echo rm) -rf ...`)
- `bash -c` nesting

(These should be weighted toward high-signal, common bypasses; avoid building a giant speculative list that creates noisy tests.)

### 4. False Positive Tests

Ensure we DON'T block legitimate uses:

- documentation containing command examples
- grep/rg patterns searching for dangerous commands
- `bd create` descriptions that mention dangerous patterns

### 5. Performance Tests

- Tier 1 trigger latency
- extraction latency
- AST match latency (bounded)
- large heredoc handling (size limits)

## Test infrastructure sketch

```
tests/
  fixtures/
    heredocs/
    bypass_attempts/
    false_positives/
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- Test suite is structured so failures are easy to attribute (tier1/extract/lang/match).
- Includes a curated corpus that covers:
  - main heredoc forms
  - at least one example per supported language
  - a small set of realistic bypass attempts
  - a strong false-positive regression set
- Enforces policy behaviors:
  - hook fail-open on parse/timeout
  - severity mapping (deny vs warn)
- Provides clear logs/assert messages so CI failures are actionable.
","notes":"Added focused heredoc coverage: unit tests for <<~ indent-stripped extraction, quoted delimiter tracking, interpreter-prefix + shebang-based language detection; evaluator integration tests for heredoc deny/allow behavior + allowlist override; quality gates passed (cargo fmt/check/clippy/test + scripts/e2e_test.sh: 176/176).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonFalcon","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:40:09.282575411-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T07:06:42.366531098-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T07:06:42.366531098-05:00","close_reason":"Completed (comprehensive heredoc unit+integration tests; all gates green)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-8wt","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:40:16.212012296-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-8wt","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-unit","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T19:37:43.125748306-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-8wt","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-unit","type":"supersedes","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:15:31.580734421-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-8wt","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-yza","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:49:53.774088173-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.3","title":"pre-commit: E2E fixture repo + detailed logging","description":"## Objective

Add an end-to-end test that:

- creates a small fixture repo
- stages files with known findings
- runs `dcg scan --staged`
- asserts output summary and exit code

Logging must include per finding:

- file:line
- extracted command
- rule id
- reason

## Design notes

- The fixture repo should include at least:
  - a safe file (no findings)
  - a file with an executable destructive command in an executed context
  - a file with a *data-only* mention of a destructive string (should not be flagged)
- The test should assert both:
  - correctness (findings exist where expected)
  - ergonomics (output is actionable and stable)

## Acceptance Criteria

- Test creates a temp git repo, stages files, runs `dcg scan --staged`, and asserts:
  - exit code matches `--fail-on` policy
  - output contains stable rule IDs and file:line locations
  - extracted commands are shown (redacted/truncated per policy)
- Test includes at least one negative fixture ensuring data-only mentions do not produce findings.
- Test output includes enough logging to debug failures without re-running locally.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.4","title":"pre-commit: docs + recommended rollout","description":"## Objective

Document how to adopt `dcg scan` in a pre-commit workflow **safely** (low-noise rollout that preserves developer/agent velocity).

## Required doc sections

### 1) What this is (and is not)

- `dcg` hook protects *interactive execution*.
- `dcg scan` protects repos by scanning *executable contexts* in files.
- Emphasize: extractor-based scanning (NOT naive grep) to avoid false positives.

### 2) Recommended rollout plan (warn-first)

- Start with:
  - narrow include set (workflows + Dockerfiles + Makefiles)
  - `--fail-on error` only
  - warnings informational for 1–2 weeks

- Expand gradually:
  - add `.sh` scripts
  - add additional extractors (compose, Terraform, GitLab CI, package.json)

### 3) How to interpret findings

- Meaning of `decision` and `severity`.
- How to read `rule_id` and why it is stable.

### 4) How to fix a finding

- Run `dcg explain "..."` for the extracted command.
- Prefer changing code to safer alternatives.
- If it is a false positive:
  - use `dcg allow` / `dcg allowlist add ...` (project or user)
  - explain allowlist safety constraints (expiration, risk_ack)

### 5) Privacy / secrets

- Recommend redaction defaults.
- Avoid printing full extracted commands in CI logs.

### 6) Example configurations

- `.dcg/hooks.toml` example for staged scanning.
- Example pre-commit hook snippet (and optional managers: husky/lefthook).

## Acceptance criteria

- A user can adopt scan-precommit without reading source.
- Docs explicitly warn against turning on warning-as-fail immediately.
- Docs show exactly how to resolve false positives safely.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:18:10.920878487-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T22:30:51.834974021-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:18:34.725455653-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:25:41.044919711-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:25:46.278526462-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5","title":"CI integration for dcg scan (PR diff scanning + PR summary)","description":"## Objective

Provide a "team-wide" safety net by running `dcg scan` in CI for pull requests.

## Background

Local hooks are not always installed or are bypassed (`--no-verify`). CI is where:

- the org can enforce safety rules
- results can be reviewed centrally
- rollouts can start in warn-only mode (avoid blocking velocity)

## Proposed MVP (GitHub Actions)

### 1) Workflow snippet (no marketplace action yet)

- A documented GitHub Actions workflow that:
  - checks out full history (diff computation)
  - builds `dcg` (or downloads release artifact later)
  - runs `dcg scan --git-diff <base>...HEAD --format json --fail-on error`
  - uploads the JSON artifact

### 2) Human-readable PR summary

- Render a concise Markdown summary to the job log (and optionally as a PR comment):
  - top N findings, grouped by file
  - per finding: line, extracted command (truncated), rule id, reason, suggestion
  - totals by severity (error/warn/info)

### 3) Rollout model

- Default recommendation:
  - start with `--fail-on error` only
  - treat warnings as advisory for 1-2 weeks
  - tighten after allowlists are created

## Output requirements

- JSON schema must be stable:
  - include `file`, `line`, `extractor_id`, `extracted_command`, `decision`, `rule_id`, `reason`, `suggestion?`
- CI mode should have deterministic ordering.

## Security/privacy

- Provide a `--redact` option (or config) for commands that might contain secrets.
- CI should avoid printing entire extracted commands if they are very long.

## Acceptance criteria

- `dcg scan --git-diff ...` handles empty diffs cleanly (0 findings, exit 0).
- A workflow snippet exists in docs or templates and is proven to run in this repo.
- When errors exist, CI fails with an actionable summary.
- When warnings exist (and `--fail-on error`), CI passes but prints the warnings clearly.
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive controls
- CI should default to diff-only scanning and warn-first rollout.
- PR comments must clearly show extracted executed commands so reviewers can judge signal.

### Tests
- `git_safety_guard-scan.5.3` covers `--git-diff` integration behavior.
- Add at least one end-to-end CI fixture that includes:
  - a true positive in an executed context
  - a data-only dangerous string that must not be flagged","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:56:31.299265569-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T23:28:51.436024163-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:56:40.548508411-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:23:47.19945344-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:23:52.295002514-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

- Make it easy to scope to diff-only files to avoid noise on large repos.

### Tests
- Validate the workflow in `git_safety_guard-scan.5.3` by running against a fixture repo in CI.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:18:47.608110165-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T23:29:02.572818656-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:19:07.365697932-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:26:00.560850741-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.2","title":"CI: optional PR comment renderer (top findings)","description":"## Objective

Optionally render a concise Markdown summary suitable for PR comments.

## Requirements

- Input: scan JSON schema v1.
- Output:
  - grouped by file
  - severity-first ordering (error, warning, info)
  - include per finding:
    - file:line
    - rule_id
    - short reason
    - truncated/redacted command preview
    - suggestion (if available)

## Markdown UX

- Use `<details><summary>` blocks per file to keep comments compact.
- Hard cap output size:
  - top N findings overall
  - per-file top M

## Determinism

- Sorting rules are deterministic and documented.

## Acceptance criteria

- Renderer output is deterministic and test-covered.
- Renderer never prints unredacted full commands when redaction is enabled.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:18:52.707720678-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T22:23:20.976031442-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:19:12.458474915-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:26:05.839049463-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:26:11.120494138-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.3","title":"CI: scan --git-diff integration tests","description":"## Objective

Add integration tests ensuring `dcg scan --git-diff`:

- returns deterministic results
- handles empty diffs
- handles renamed/moved files gracefully

## Logging requirements

- Tests should log the diff range and files considered.

## Test plan

- Create a temp repo with two commits.
- Modify files across commits to cover:
  - added file
  - modified file
  - renamed file
  - deleted file (should be skipped gracefully)
- Run `dcg scan --git-diff <base>..<head>` and assert:
  - deterministic ordering
  - stable JSON (if using `--json`)
  - correct exit code per `--fail-on`

## Acceptance Criteria

- `dcg scan --git-diff <range>` succeeds on:
  - empty diffs (exit 0, no findings)
  - renames/moves (scans new path, no crash)
  - deletes (skips missing paths, no crash)
- Output is deterministic across runs.
- Tests emit enough logs (diff range, candidate files, filtered files) to debug failures in CI.
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive regression
Include a negative fixture in the diff-range test:

- a file where a dangerous string appears only in docs/comments and must not produce a finding

This prevents CI adoption from being derailed by noisy PR checks.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:18:57.803468576-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T23:29:13.151737325-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:19:17.551755437-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:26:16.363785468-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:26:21.594148009-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:26:26.844970733-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.4","title":"Optional: package dcg scan as a reusable GitHub Action","description":"## Objective

Package `dcg scan` into a reusable GitHub Action for easier org-scale adoption.

## Why optional

A workflow snippet is enough to start. A marketplace action is accretive once:

- the scan JSON schema is stable
- allowlisting workflow is mature
- false positives are under control

## Requirements

- Action inputs:
  - `fail_on`
  - `scan_paths` or diff mode
  - `comment_on_pr`
- Action outputs:
  - summary counts
  - artifact upload

## Acceptance criteria

- Example repo can adopt by adding a few lines.
- Action pins a specific dcg version (no surprise upgrades).
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### Tests
- Validate the action packaging with an end-to-end workflow in a fixture repo:
  - ensures correct exit codes
  - ensures deterministic output
